hints.text: The goal: $\Box(A \land B) \to (\Box A \land \Box B)$. You'll need to prove two tautologies under empty context, apply NEC to box them, assume $\Box(A \land B)$, then apply the K axiom twice. Use `(box-intro N)` only when the context is empty — the tautologies do that for you.
First, prove $(A \land B) \to A$. Assume the conjunction, extract $A$ with `and-elim-l`, and discharge with `->-intro`.
With empty context, apply NEC: `(box-intro 3)` to box your first tautology.
Now assume the main antecedent: $\Box(A \land B)$.
K axiom: `(box-elim 4 5)` — from $\Box((A \land B) \to A)$ and $\Box(A \land B)$, derive $\Box A$.
Repeat for the right half: prove $(A \land B) \to B$ (assume, `and-elim-r`, discharge), then `(box-intro)` and `(box-elim)` with your assumption at step 5.
